description:
Add the plugin's config type and the projection declaration that the
participant protocol's describe handshake will surface (when balls core
calls describe). Config fields:
- repo: owner/name (required)
- target_label: optional label filter for pull (only mirror issues
  carrying this label; absent = all issues)
- on_external_delete: "deferred" | "closed" | "noop" (default: deferred)
- close_mirror: "authoritative" | "best_effort" | "off"
  (default: authoritative per Epic B decision)
- api_base: optional, default https://api.github.com

Projection: owns `external.github_issues.*`; reads task.title,
task.description, task.status (for mirroring outbound). Documented
overlap with task.status only when close_mirror != "off".

WHY: the projection is the contract with balls core. If two participants
claim ownership of the same field, the participant registry rejects them
at config validation (SPEC §17 conformance test 3). Declaring projection
explicitly here — before any push/pull behavior — is what makes B3-B5
implementable without re-litigating ownership at every command.

ACCEPTANCE:
- config.rs in the issues crate (depends on shared::config_base for the
  cross-cutting api_base/repo/owner-name validation).
- Projection declaration constants are exported; a unit test asserts
  disjointness from external.github.* (forge plugin's projection) by
  string comparison.
- Default close_mirror is "authoritative"; opting out requires explicit
  config. Documented in the plugin README B7 ships.
- 100% coverage; under 300 lines per file.
title: Config schema + external.github_issues.* projection declaration
